title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Edward recalls a childhood memory of his mother forcing him to hold his grandfather's hand one last time before he died.

Later, at the Brawnshan Hotel, Poirot and Edward enter a lavish restaurant where Luca Lazari approaches them to apologize for spreading rumors and misinterpreting messages. During the meal, a short, stout man named Raphael Bork admits to being the server responsible for the victims. Bork explains that at 7:15 PM on Thursday, he delivered "dinner-time tea" to Room 317, which contained tea, various sandwiches, a mixed cake set, and scones with jam and cream for Harriet Sipper, Edith Gainsborough, and Richard Nigges. Bork explicitly states that he did not deliver any sherry to Mr. Nigges.

Edward identifies a discrepancy: while the tea cups in Harriet Sipple's room matched Bork's delivery, a sherry glass was found in Richard Nigges's room despite no staff member admitting to delivering it. Lazari confirms the glass belongs to the hotel, which is highly unusual.

Poirot questions Bork about the victims' behavior. Bork describes the trio as "sneaky" and laughing, noting they were gossiping about a story involving an old woman and a young man. Poirot observes that the three victims were friends who were unaware of their impending deaths. As Poirot reflects on this, a pale, black-haired young man at a nearby table jumps up with a terrified expression, appearing eager to speak.
